threat_model: An operator shadow-pilot evidence intake packet could be mistaken for collected operator evidence or runtime promotion approval if it allows collected-observation flags, parity confirmation, promotion readiness, or terminal closure claims. The mitigation is a schema-backed AwaitingEvidence fixture with hard false authority fields, explicit unknown observation refs, promotion gates that continue to block runtime promotion, and focused tests for authority and evidence-drift rejection.

required_checks.check: authority boundary check proves the operator evidence packet keeps runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, and terminal closure false
policy bypass check proves collected observations and promotion-ready flags remain rejected until a separate operator witness exists
receipt integrity check proves the checked-in fixture matches the deterministic produced packet and validates against the schema
audit visibility check records one AwaitingEvidence observation placeholder for every expected shadow dossier plan class

findings.mitigation: The schema and validator hard-deny runtime_promotion_authorized, execution_allowed, dispatch_allowed, runtime_replanning_enabled, success_claim_allowed, and terminal_closure.
The fixture remains AwaitingEvidence, all observation refs are unknown refs, and collected-observation drift is rejected.

mitigations: keep the evidence packet local, deterministic, and read-only
hard-deny all runtime promotion and terminal closure fields in the schema
bind the packet to the deterministic shadow dossier id and hash
record plan-class observation placeholders without claiming any observation has been collected
